game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Leps World Jump N Run Games** Players control Lep the leprechaun in a 2D side-scrolling platformer, navigating through 160 levels across 6 distinct world themes. Movement is controlled via a virtual D-pad on the left side of the screen, while a dedicated jump button on the right allows players to tap for a standard jump or hold for a higher leap. Lep's objective is to reach the flag at the end of each level, collecting scattered gold coins for score and cloverleaves to restore one of his three health points, while avoiding or defeating 9 unique enemy types by jumping on their heads. Levels progressively introduce more complex platforming challenges, increased enemy density, and environmental hazards like bottomless pits, with losing all health or falling into a pit resulting in a level restart. Successfully completing levels unlocks subsequent stages, and players can aim to collect all gold coins within a level to achieve a perfect score or unlock bonus content.
game_info.description: Guide Lep the leprechaun through magical platforming worlds! Navigate treacherous levels filled with enemies, collect shimmering gold coins for points, and gather healing cloverleaves to restore your health. Jump on enemy heads to defeat them, but beware of falling into pits or losing all your health. Reach the flag at the end of each level to progress through increasingly challenging stages across mystical realms.
game_info.controls:
A/D Keys: Move left/right
W Key: Jump (tap for normal jump, hold for higher jump)
Arrow Keys: Alternative movement (←/→ move, ↑ jumps)
Space: Jump
Enter: Start game
Escape: Pause/unpause
R: Restart (when game over)

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic player movement and jumping mechanics. This ensures the leprechaun can move left/right and perform both normal and high jumps correctly with proper physics response.
TEST_1.strategy_description: The test will automatically move the player left and right across the level, perform normal jumps and high jumps at regular intervals. It will verify that movement controls are responsive and jumping mechanics work as expected.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Player should move smoothly left and right, jump normally when tapping jump, and jump higher when holding jump longer. All movement should feel responsive and physics-based. Test succeeds when all movement controls function properly.

TEST_1.expected: Player should move smoothly left and right, jump normally when tapping jump, and jump higher when holding jump longer. All movement should feel responsive and physics-based. Test succeeds when all movement controls function properly.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing win condition by automatically navigating to the level end flag. This verifies the level completion logic and progression system work correctly.
TEST_2.strategy_description: The test will automatically move the player toward the flag at the end of the level, jumping over obstacles and enemies as needed. It will attempt to reach the flag to trigger the win condition.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Player should successfully reach the flag, trigger the level completion, and display the win screen. The game should properly transition to the GAME_OVER_WIN state and show appropriate victory message with restart option.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing enemy collision and defeat mechanics. This ensures jumping on enemies defeats them while touching them from the side causes damage to the player.
TEST_3.strategy_description: The test will move the player to encounter enemies, attempt to jump on their heads to defeat them, and also test side collisions to verify damage is taken correctly.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Jumping on enemy heads should defeat them and potentially award points. Side collisions should reduce player health. Player should lose health appropriately and game should end when health reaches zero.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing collectible items including gold coins and cloverleaves. This verifies the collection system works and cloverleaves restore health properly.
TEST_4.strategy_description: The test will navigate the player to collect gold coins and cloverleaves, monitoring score increases and health restoration respectively.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Gold coins should be collected and increase the score. Cloverleaves should be collected and restore one health point up to the maximum. Collection should be visually and mechanically confirmed.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing fall damage and pit mechanics. This ensures falling into pits or off the level causes appropriate game over conditions.
TEST_5.strategy_description: The test will intentionally move the player to fall off platforms or into pits to trigger the fall-based game over condition.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Falling into pits or off the bottom of the level should immediately trigger game over. The game should transition to GAME_OVER_LOSE state and display appropriate failure message with restart option.
